mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where unemployment rises > 0.5pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal income and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where INDPRO increases by > 1% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 300.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 increases correspond to negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and reduced economic activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months where INDPRO falls by more than 0.5%.
